title: Rate Limiting Middleware

summary.overview: This PR adds a token bucket rate limiting middleware to the API gateway with per-client and per-endpoint granularity. The implementation uses a distributed Redis-backed store for rate limit counters, ensuring consistent enforcement across multiple gateway instances. Code quality is excellent with thorough configuration options and clean separation of concerns.

riskAssessment.summary: The rate limiting middleware operates as a non-destructive filter in the request pipeline. Redis failure is handled gracefully with a fallback to allow-all behavior, and the middleware can be bypassed via configuration without code changes.
items: Redis connectivity issues will degrade to open-allow mode, which is an intentional design choice but should be monitored
Rate limit headers are added to all responses, which could expose internal capacity information to external consumers

strengths: Token bucket algorithm is implemented correctly with atomic Redis operations using Lua scripts to prevent race conditions
Comprehensive configuration model supports per-endpoint, per-client, and global rate limits with sensible defaults
Middleware gracefully degrades to pass-through on Redis failures, preventing rate limiter outages from cascading to API availability
Test suite covers edge cases including concurrent requests, Redis timeouts, and configuration hot-reload scenarios
Clean use of the Options pattern for configuration binding with validation attributes on all required fields

issues.message: The Retry-After header value is calculated using DateTime.UtcNow which may drift slightly from the Redis server's clock, potentially providing inaccurate retry guidance to clients.
issues.recommendation: Use the TTL returned from the Redis PTTL command for the rate limit key to calculate the Retry-After value, ensuring consistency with the actual token bucket refill timing.

recommendations: Add metrics emission for rate limit hits and misses to enable dashboarding and alerting on throttling patterns
Consider implementing a sliding window log algorithm as an alternative strategy for endpoints requiring stricter burst control
Document the rate limit response headers and error codes in the API gateway's public-facing developer documentation
